title: \TRANSPARENT=1 prevents BRAM inference if $memrd and $memwr are in asynchronous domains
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Use [x.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/3637380/x.zip). Run `yosys x.il -p 'synth_ice40 -run :map_ffram'`.

## Expected behavior

A BRAM is inferred, and the `\TRANSPARENT` parameter is ignored. As @cliffordwolf said elsewhere:
> If there are two (non-synchronized) clock domains then TRANSPARENT is only relevant for write ports that are in the same clock domain as the transparent read port.

## Actual behavior

No BRAM is inferred:
```
    Mapping to bram type $__ICE40_RAM4K_M0 (variant 1):
      Write port #0 is in clock domain \write_clk.
        Mapped to bram port B1.
      Read port #0 is in clock domain \read_clk.
        Bram port A1.1 cannot have soft transparency logic added as read and write clock domains differ.
        Failed to map read port #0.
    Mapping to bram type $__ICE40_RAM4K_M0 failed.
```
code_blocks:
Mapping to bram type $__ICE40_RAM4K_M0 (variant 1):
      Write port #0 is in clock domain \write_clk.
        Mapped to bram port B1.
      Read port #0 is in clock domain \read_clk.
        Bram port A1.1 cannot have soft transparency logic added as read and write clock domains differ.
        Failed to map read port #0.
    Mapping to bram type $__ICE40_RAM4K_M0 failed.
